config.prompt: Enter plan mode. Design a plan for creating a hello world script in TypeScript. Then call ExitPlanMode to present the plan for my approval.

allowedPrompts.prompt: run the hello.ts script via npx tsx to verify output

tool_input.plan:
# Hello World TypeScript Script

## Context
The user wants a minimal TypeScript "hello world" script in the current working directory (`/private/tmp`). This is not a git repository and has no existing project setup, so the plan creates a single standalone `.ts` file that can be run with `npx tsx` (no install/build step needed).

## Approach
Create one file: `/private/tmp/hello.ts` containing a single `console.log("Hello, world!");` statement.

No `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, or `node_modules` will be added — keeping the artifact to one file matches the scope of the request.

## Files to create
- `/private/tmp/hello.ts` — the script

## Verification
Run the script with one of:
- `npx tsx /private/tmp/hello.ts` (no install required)
- `npx ts-node /private/tmp/hello.ts`

Expected output:
```
Hello, world!
```

payload.planText:
# Hello World TypeScript Script

## Context
The user wants a minimal TypeScript "hello world" script in the current working directory (`/private/tmp`). This is not a git repository and has no existing project setup, so the plan creates a single standalone `.ts` file that can be run with `npx tsx` (no install/build step needed).

## Approach
Create one file: `/private/tmp/hello.ts` containing a single `console.log("Hello, world!");` statement.

No `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, or `node_modules` will be added — keeping the artifact to one file matches the scope of the request.

## Files to create
- `/private/tmp/hello.ts` — the script

## Verification
Run the script with one of:
- `npx tsx /private/tmp/hello.ts` (no install required)
- `npx ts-node /private/tmp/hello.ts`

Expected output:
```
Hello, world!
```
